creator: john.c.prather@gmail.com
text:
I had actually finished a port of pithos and was going to submit it
back in October when I saw that this one was submitted just hours
earlier and was far more thorough than mine (plus mine was written by
someone who doesn't understand these python distutils and egginfo
things), so I've got some interest in seeing this PR make it into the
ports tree.

To address the distfiles for this port, I had actually exported the
current revision from the repo and archived it into a distfile to
stick on my webhosting server, for lack of any better plans.

As seen in my crappy attempt at a port of pithos at
http://interwebfun.org/ports/pithos/ the distfile would have to be
re-rolled with each port update (compared to the effort of prepping
the port for a new version, rolling a distfile is a rather simple
task).  Unfortunately, without official releases, it may be hard to
say when it's time to make an upgrade available, short of carefully
monitoring commits to pithos.

I'm pretty sure there may be a better way to handle software where the
upstream developers do not provide actual release files, but I haven't
figured it out yet.

Hopefully some of this helps figure out how to get this PR moving.  I
use pithos almost every day and would love to see it committed!
